findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "spala_help" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Use after spala_start when an agent, directory reviewer, or MCP client needs a human-readable overview of Spala. Explains Spala as an AI-assisted backend platfo…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "spala_help" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Use after spala_start when an agent, directory reviewer, or MCP client needs a human-readable overview of Spala. Explains Spala as an AI-assisted backend platfo…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "account_status" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

description: AUTH REQUIRED. List projects in an authoritative organization returned for the authenticated Spala user. Provide organizationId when the authenticated user has multiple organizations; a sole organization is selected automatically. Invalid subject credentials receive an OAuth 401 challenge; missing a

labels.name: project_create
labels.description: AUTH REQUIRED; WRITES TO THE SPALA CONTROL PLANE. Create a real project with project_name and an authoritative organization_id. Provide organizationId when the authenticated user has multiple organizations; a sole organization is selected automatically. This operation is not read-only or idempotent.

labels.name: project_connect
labels.description: AUTH REQUIRED; IDEMPOTENT PROJECT CONNECTION WRITE. Prepare exactly one authenticated project for agent access. Provide one installer client (codex, roo). When omitted, returns client_selection_required without executable argv. The control plane verifies account and project access, then the public M

labels.name: project_select
labels.description: Compatibility alias for project_connect. AUTH REQUIRED; IDEMPOTENT PROJECT CONNECTION WRITE. Enables project MCP when needed and returns the same workspace-only exact-URL binding plan with one-time installer bootstrap. Agents must never infer project MCP URLs from subdomains or hosts or install proj

labels.name: project_get_mcp_manifest
labels.description: AUTH REQUIRED; IDEMPOTENT PROJECT CONNECTION WRITE. Prepare one authenticated project and return its exact platform-provided project MCP manifestUrl, mcpUrl, and executable workspace binding plan. Provide one installer client (codex, roo). When omitted, returns client_selection_required without exec

labels.name: project_get_public_context
labels.description: AUTH REQUIRED; READ-ONLY. Return the documented MCP handoff fields for one authenticated project without exposing tokens, private source code, or unrelated customer data. Does not require an installer client and does not return executable installer argv.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: spala_get_tool_map, docs_search, addon_list, project_list, project_get_mcp_manifest · Private data: spala_help, template_list, account_status, project_list, project_get_public_context · Public sink: spala_help, addon_list.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: spala_get_tool_map, docs_search, addon_list, project_list, project_get_mcp_manifest · Private data: spala_help, template_list, account_status, project_list, project_get_public_context · Public sink: spala_help, addon_list

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
